info.title: FlowSavvy API
info.description: The FlowSavvy API is in beta and is actively evolving. While the API is in beta, breaking changes may occur without warning as we ship improvements. Please submit feedback via the in-app help center or email us at support@flowsavvy.app.

get.description: Returns events and tasks, ordered by id.

parameters.name: modifiedAfter
parameters.description: When set, only items with `lastModified` strictly after this instant are returned, enabling incremental syncing. ISO 8601 format in UTC (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ)

schema.description: When set, only items with `lastModified` strictly after this instant are returned, enabling incremental syncing. ISO 8601 format in UTC (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ)

parameters.name: itemType
parameters.description: When set, only items of that type are returned.

parameters.name: completed
parameters.description: Only items with this completion status are returned.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description:
When set, only tasks in that list are returned; Use `inbox` for the inbox.
`itemType` must be `task`.

parameters.name: calendarId
parameters.description:
When set, only events in that calendar are returned.
`itemType` must be `event`.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: When set, only items whose title, notes, or location contain the value (case-insensitive) are returned

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Token specifying which result page to return. Optional.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum items per response. Allowed range is 1–200; default is 50.

get.description: Get a single event or task by id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Item id

parameters.name: occurrenceDate
parameters.description:
Optional. Selects one occurrence of a repeating series. Pass `yyyy-MM-dd`.
For repeating events and fixed-time tasks, use the date component of that occurrence's `startDateTime`;
For repeating auto-scheduled tasks, use the date component of that occurrence's `dueDateTime`.
If that occurrence is stored as its own item, that item is returned. Otherwise the response describes that occurrence
(still under the parent id) so you can update it with PUT and the same occurrenceDate query.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Item id

parameters.name: occurrenceDate
scope
parameters.description: Required for repeating items; controls whether the delete applies to just the specified occurrence or to all future occurrences.

put.description:
Replaces a task with the request body. To update a task, get the current task first, 
change properties, then send the whole task back. Partial PATCH-style bodies are NOT 
supported. Omitted fields are cleared or reset.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Task id

parameters.name: occurrenceDate
scope
parameters.description: Required for repeating tasks; controls whether the update applies to just the specified occurrence or to all future occurrences.

put.description:
Replaces an event with the request body. To update an event, get the current event first, 
change properties, then send the whole event back. Partial PATCH-style bodies are NOT 
supported. Omitted fields are cleared or reset.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Event id

parameters.name: occurrenceDate
parameters.description:
Only for repeating events. For a repeating event, which occurrence to edit, as `yyyy-MM-dd`,
matching the date component of that occurrence's `startDateTime`.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Required for repeating events; controls whether the update applies to just the specified occurrence or to all future occurrences.

post.description:
Marks a task complete. If the task is already completed, the response is still `200` with the current item.
Completing an occurrence of a repeating task creates a new task for that occurrence that's completely separate from the parent series.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Task id

parameters.name: occurrenceDate
parameters.description:
For repeating tasks only. Identifies an occurrence out of the repeating series. Formatted as `yyyy-MM-dd`.
For repeating fixed-time tasks, the date component of that occurrence's `startDateTime`;
For repeating auto-scheduled tasks, the date component of that occurrence's `dueDateTime`.
Omit to complete the first occurrence.

post.description: Marks a task incomplete. If the task is already incomplete, the response is still `204`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Task id

get.description: Get schedule entries within a date range. Max date range is 31 days.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Inclusive range start date in the time zone set in the user's settings. Use yyyy-MM-dd.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Inclusive range end date in the time zone set in the user's settings. Use yyyy-MM-dd.

post.description:
Runs FlowSavvy's auto-scheduling engine and reschedules auto-scheduled tasks.
Call this after making changes that might affect the auto-scheduling such as 
creating, updating, or deleting items.

parameters.name: reschedulePastTasks
parameters.description:
When true, all tasks, including past and in-progress tasks, get rescheduled.
When false, past and in-progress auto-scheduled time blocks stay put while the rest of the schedule is recalculated.

color.description: Calendar display color in hex format (e.g. "#FF0000").

hidden.description: Indicates whether the events in this calendar are visible on the schedule view.

source.description: Calendar provider identifier. Possible values are `flowsavvy`, `google`, `outlook`, or `icloud`.

sourceUrl.description: When this calendar is subscribed via a web feed, the ICS (or equivalent) URL; otherwise `null`.

busy.description:
Indicates busy/free availability. Defaults to `false` for all-day events and
`true` for regular events.

calendarId.description: The id of the calendar this event belongs to. Omit to set to the user's default calendar.

endDateTime.description:
End of the event. This date/time is exclusive, so for all-day events, this is 
12am on the day after the last day of the event. For example, an all-day event 
on May 10 would have an endDateTime of May 11 at 12am. ISO 8601 format without offset (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss)

startDateTime.description: Start of the event. For all-day events, always uses midnight. ISO 8601 format without offset (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss)

itemType.description: Discriminator for item subtype. Can be `task` or `event`.

bufferAfterMinutes.description: Buffer after this item in minutes. Defaults to the value set in event and task settings.

bufferBeforeMinutes.description: Buffer before this item in minutes. Defaults to the value set in event and task settings.

cancelledOccurrenceDates.description: For repeating items only, dates where an occurrence was deleted.

customColor.description:
Event/task display color override in hex format (e.g. "#FF0000").
Null means "use calendar/list/default color behavior."

notes.description:
Rich-text HTML string. Typical tags include paragraphs, inline formatting, lists, and links. Examples: `

Call Alex

`, `

Important: bring docs

`, `
  • First
  • Second
`. Truncated to 8192 chars during validation.

occurrenceDate.description:
When this item is an edited occurrence of a repeating series, this date identifies where the occurrence was originally in the repeating series.
For repeating events and fixed-time tasks, this is the date component of the occurrence's original `startDateTime`.
For repeating auto-scheduled tasks, this is the date component of the occurrence's original `dueDateTime`.

parentItemId.description:
Points to the ID of this item's parent when this item is an edited occurrence of a recurring item.
If null, this item is not an edited occurrence and represents the main/original item.

recurrenceRule.description: RRULE string without the `RRULE:` prefix (e.g. FREQ=DAILY;INTERVAL=1). Null means non-repeating.

timeZone.description:
Can be an IANA time zone id or `Floating`, which follows the user's time zone when it changes.
Set to null to apply the user's "time zone default" setting.

nextPageToken.description: Token used to access the next page of this result. Omitted if no further results are available.

color.description:
The list's display color in hex format (e.g. "#FF0000").
Tasks in this list use this color when the global task color setting is "Use list color"
and the task has no custom color override.

defaultListId.description: The user's default task list id, or `null` for the inbox.

completed.description:
Whether the underlying item is fully completed.
This differs from `thisPartCompleted`, which only refers to this specific schedule block.

isAutoScheduled.description: Indicates whether this block was generated for an auto-scheduled task.

itemType.description: Indicates whether the schedule entry belongs to a `task` or an `event`.

occurrenceDate.description:
For repeating items only. This date identifies the occurrence in a repeating series.
For repeating events and fixed-time tasks, this is the date component of the occurrence's `startDateTime`.
For repeating auto-scheduled tasks, this is the date component of the occurrence's `dueDateTime`.

ScheduleItem.description: Represents one rendered segment on the calendar and may be one part of a larger event or task.

items.description: Represents one rendered segment on the calendar and may be one part of a larger event or task.

recurringHours.description: Recurring weekly availability keyed by weekday (`sunday` … `saturday`).

dateOverrides.description:
Date-specific overrides keyed by calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD). Dates not listed use `recurringHours`.
An empty array means that date is explicitly blocked.

defaultSchedulingHoursId.description: The user's default scheduling hours id.

blockedByIds.description: IDs of items that this task must be scheduled after.

blocksIds.description: IDs of items that this task must be scheduled before.

canBeStartedAt.description:
Only applies to auto-scheduled tasks. Indicates when work can begin.
For non-repeating tasks, null means "can be started now".
For repeating tasks, the time span between `canBeStartedAt` and `dueDateTime`
defines how early each occurrence can be started before its due date. ISO 8601 format without offset (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss)

dueDateTime.description: The date/time the task should be scheduled before. Required for repeating auto-scheduled tasks since the due date is what repeats. ISO 8601 format without offset (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss)

durationMinutes.description: Only applies to auto-scheduled tasks. Tasks must be less than 24 hours long.

endDateTime.description: Only applies to fixed-time tasks. Tasks must be less than 24 hours long. ISO 8601 format without offset (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss)

isAutoScheduled.description: When `true`, FlowSavvy auto-schedules the task based on duration, due date, scheduling hours, and more. When `false`, the task is a fixed-time task pinned to a specific time using `startDateTime` and `endDateTime` instead. Tasks in the inbox (`listId` is `inbox`) are never scheduled. When omitted, presence of `startDateTime` implies fixed-time, and absence of `startDateTime` implies auto-scheduled.

listId.description:
The ID of the task list this task belongs to. `inbox` means the task is in the inbox. 
Tasks in the inbox do not get scheduled. Omit to set to the user's default list.

minLengthMinutes.description:
Minimum length in minutes of any scheduled chunk of this task.
Only applies to auto-scheduled tasks. Set to the full duration to disable splitting.
When omitted, this value gets set using the values for "Splittable" and "Min duration"
in "Events and tasks" settings and the task's duration.

progressMinutes.description: Completed progress in minutes. Ignored when creating a task.

schedulingHoursId.description:
The id of the scheduling hours during which this task can be scheduled. Only applies to auto-scheduled tasks.
Omit to set to the list's default scheduling hours.

startDateTime.description: Only applies to fixed-time tasks. ISO 8601 format without offset (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss)

startTime.description: Start of the window in local time (HH:mm).

endTime.description: End of the window in local time (HH:mm). `00:00` means 12am the next day.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
All API requests must include either your **API key** or an **OAuth access token** in the `Authorization` header using the **Bearer** scheme.
API keys can be viewed or regenerated from **Settings → Integrations → API** in the FlowSavvy app (requires Pro).
```http
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

tags.name: MCP
tags.description:
FlowSavvy exposes an **MCP** server at the following URL:
```http
https://my.flowsavvy.app/mcp
```
The server supports all the same operations as the API and uses **OAuth** for authentication instead of your API key.
